verification_gate: Every current control-plane column, relation, proof URI, lifecycle transition, North Star anchor, and source provenance has a database owner and deterministic projection contract with no information loss.

every_control_plane_column_has_database_.owners: 17 task_graph columns -> task_node + 5 ordinal child tables (task_dependency, task_input, task_target_artifact, task_allowed_path, task_blocked_path); 8 coverage columns -> ns_anchor + anchor_coverage; proof-record, proof-ledger, task-family, source-registry (16 cols), adopted-capabilities (12 cols), and store-decision fields each mapped to a named owning column

every_relation_has_database_owner.single_writer_invariant: PostgreSQL owns each relation exactly once (STORE-001 invariant 1); redb never a second durable writer

every_proof_uri_has_database_owner.owner: proof_record keyed by (task_id, revision) + proof_ledger_entry keyed by sequence with proof_sha256 pinning content identity; source-extract proof_uris (NBSTATED family) owned by the source relation
every_proof_uri_has_database_owner.integrity_rule: proof_uri -> proof_records/*.proof.json requires a proof_record whose recomputed SHA-256 equals proof_ledger_entry.proof_sha256

every_lifecycle_transition_has_database_.history_preserved: append-only; proof history never rewritten (blocked_paths honored)

every_north_star_anchor_has_database_own.owner: ns_anchor (insert-once; anchor_id + required_outcome frozen per NORTH_STAR.md lock 2026-07-10) + anchor_coverage for anchor->task causal edges

every_source_provenance_has_database_own.owner: source (content_sha256) + source_task_map + source_claim + claim_evidence + adopted_capability (source_commit_or_pr, current_path, ci_receipt, parity_gate) + store_decision + store_class_assignment + store_invariant
every_source_provenance_has_database_own.identity_rule: content_sha256 / proof_sha256 / source_commit_or_pr preserved; projection rejected if recomputed hash != stored identity (STORE-001 invariant 5)

determinism_rules: stable ORDER BY on persisted ordinal key (source_row_number, anchor_ordinal, sequence)
CSV RFC4180 QUOTE_ALL / UTF-8 / LF / verbatim header
deterministic_projection_contract.engine: PostgreSQL -> envctl materialize -> filesystem (forward-only, disposable derived class)

no_information_loss.contract: for each relation, DB -> projection -> parse -> DB is an identity (round-trip) AND projection bytes equal the committed file (checksum)

store_001_alignment.control_plane_class: durable relational macro-state -> PostgreSQL + RuVector canonical owner
store_001_alignment.projection_class: derived projections -> PostgreSQL projected by envctl (read-only, forward-only)
store_001_alignment.redb_role: transient forward buffer during one-time ingest only; never a second durable writer
store_001_alignment.codedb_role: parser/ingestion path only (parses committed surfaces -> redb buffer -> envctl -> PostgreSQL)
store_001_alignment.ruvector_role: native No-Sidecar extension hosting goal/gate/summary embeddings for hybrid SQL+vector planning queries
store_001_alignment.directive_honored: building to intended specs is never a downgrade

proof_summary: POSTGRES-003 defines the PostgreSQL-native control plane for the Planning Spine in generated/postgres_control_plane_schema_plan.json, assigning a database owner and a deterministic projection contract to every item in each of the gate's six categories. COLUMNS: all 17 task_graph.source.csv columns and all 8 north_star_coverage.source.csv columns are owned, with the seven '; '-delimited multi-value columns (parent_id, inputs, target_artifacts, allowed_paths, blocked_paths, phase_task_ids, mapped_task_ids) normalized into ordinal child tables so re-serialization is byte-identical, and only status/proof_uri/next_action left mutable to mirror task_graph.status.json update_policy exactly. RELATIONS: 31 owning PostgreSQL tables cover the task graph, coverage table, proof records, the 166-entry proof ledger, task families, the 32-row source registry, the 19-row adopted-capabilities map, and the STORE-001 decision, each owned exactly once per STORE-001 invariant 1. PROOF URIs: proof_record (task_id, revision) plus proof_ledger_entry pinning proof_sha256 own every proof URI, with source-extract URIs owned by the source relation. LIFECYCLE: the seven canonical states from task_graph.status.json.lifecycle_counts (draft 76, ready 0, running 1 GRAPH-001, simulated 0, complete 118, blocked 0, rolled-back 1 LPS-025) plus ten transition edges are owned by lifecycle_state/lifecycle_transition with append-only task_status_event and proof_ledger_entry so no history is rewritten, and the coarse authored status {Draft, Running, Complete} maps deterministically to the fine state. NORTH STAR: all five immutable anchors (NS-EXTENSION, NS-WHOLE, NS-CLARITY, NS-DIRECTION, NS-PROGRESS) are owned by ns_anchor with anchor_coverage holding the anchor->task edges; POSTGRES-003 itself is covered by NS-WHOLE, NS-CLARITY, and NS-DIRECTION. SOURCE PROVENANCE: source, source_claim, claim_evidence, adopted_capability, and store_decision own all provenance with content_sha256 and source commit/PR lineage preserved per STORE-001 invariant 5. Eleven projection contracts make the committed CSV/JSON/JSONL/MD surfaces byte-identical, round-trippable envctl projections of PostgreSQL rows (derived-projections class), and the no-information-loss contract requires each projection to both round-trip to identical rows and match the committed file checksum. Under STORE-001 the control plane is the durable relational macro-state class owned by PostgreSQL + RuVector, the surfaces are the derived-projections class projected by envctl, and redb is transient-only. No live PostgreSQL was created or mutated; the committed CSV and proof ledger remain canonical until POSTGRES-010 proves cutover; product_execution_performed is false.

live_commands.purpose: Enumerate the real control-plane sets the gate references (columns, statuses, phases, anchors, ledger entries, source/capability rows) directly from the committed surfaces so every assignment is grounded
live_commands.command: python3 (csv/json enumeration of task_graph.source.csv, task_graph.status.json, north_star_coverage.source.csv, proof_ledger.jsonl, notebooklm_source_registry.source.csv, adopted_capabilities.source.csv)
output: lifecycle_counts: draft=76 ready=0 running=1 simulated=0 complete=118 blocked=0 rolled-back=1
proof_ledger entries=166; source_registry rows=32 cols=16; adopted_capabilities rows=19 cols=12
multi-value delimiter='; ' ; csv dialect=QUOTE_ALL ; stable order key=source_row_number ; multi-parent example=LIFEOS-010
